theme.name: Lighthouse Keepers
theme.setting_summary: A windswept point. Three keepers split the night shift between them, routing oil from the cellar drums to the great lamp above.

acts.title: The Night Shift
acts.intro_text: Wind off the headland. The lamp must burn before the first hull rounds the point. Lay the lines from the cellar to the wick.
acts.outro_text: The beam swings out across the water. Far off, a fishing boat answers with a lantern flick. The night is yours.

puzzles.title: First Light
puzzles.briefing: Keeper: "Oil drum on the west wall, wick on the east. Run a line between them. The cellar's narrow — three cells across."

puzzles.title: Two Wicks
puzzles.briefing: Keeper: "The reserve lamp burns on the south rail; the main on the north. The pump alternates between them. One splitter, two outputs."

puzzles.title: The Filter Grate
puzzles.briefing: Keeper: "Cellar oil's mixed with brine on a wet night. Filter the brine out before it reaches the wick. Brine ruins the burn."

ending.good: Three nights kept, three lamps lit. The fog rolls in but the beam keeps cutting, and you keep the watch.
ending.neutral: The work is done, for now.
